paper_title: Analysis of Hydroxy Fatty Acids from the Pollen of Brassica campestris L. var. oleifera DC. by UPLC-MS/MS

methods.method_name: Structural Analysis of Hydroxy Fatty Acids
methods.description: This method involves the structural analysis of hydroxy fatty acids using MS/MS to identify molecular ions, characteristic product ions, and the configuration of olefinic bonds. It is used to determine the structure and configuration of hydroxy fatty acids in compounds.

steps.description: The molecular ion of compound 3 indicated an excess of 16 Da in comparison with that of 1, which suggested a surplus hydroxyl group in compound 2.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze molecular ion differences to suggest hydroxyl group presence

steps.description: The full MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 3 showed the characteristic product ions of m/z 239, 183, 269, 115, and 89, which were formed from the allyl scission and the -fission of OH group respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze MS/MS spectrum for characteristic product ions
inputs: MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 3

steps.description: The configuration of the olefinic bonds was deduced from the biosynthetic pathway, and the natural sources of unsaturated fatty acids and their derivatives are rich in the cis isomer, so the olefinic bonds of compound 3 were Z geometry.
steps.searchable_summary: deduce olefinic bond configuration from biosynthetic pathway
inputs: natural sources of unsaturated fatty acids

steps.description: The full MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 4 showed the characteristic product ions of m/z 221, and 223, which were formed from the allyl scission, and another major peak of m/z 251 resulted from the -fission of the OH group.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze MS/MS spectrum for compound 4 product ions
inputs: MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 4

steps.description: The configuration of the olefinic bonds was also determined from the biosynthetic pathway, and compound 4 was tentatively assigned as 15,16-dihydroxy-9Z,12Z-octadecadienoic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: determine olefinic bond configuration for compound 4

steps.description: The molecular ion of compound 5 indicated a lack of 16 Da in comparison with that of 4.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze molecular ion differences for compound 5

steps.description: The full MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 5 showed the characteristic peaks of m/z 73, 207, 221 and 251.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze MS/MS spectrum for compound 5 product ions
inputs: MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 5

steps.description: The configuration of the olefinic bonds was also determined from the biosynthetic pathway, and compound 5 was tentatively assigned as 15hydroxy-6Z,9Z,12Z-octadecatrienoic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: determine olefinic bond configuration for compound 5

steps.description: The molecular ion of compound 6 indicated a lack of 16 Da in comparison with that of 1.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze molecular ion differences for compound 6

steps.description: The full MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 6 showed the characteristic peaks of m/z 73, 167, 223, and 253.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze MS/MS spectrum for compound 6 product ions
inputs: MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 6

steps.description: The configuration of the olefinic bonds was also determined from the biosynthetic pathway, and compound 6 was tentatively assigned as 15-hydroxy-9Z,12Z-octadecadienoic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: determine olefinic bond configuration for compound 6

steps.description: The molecular ion of compound 7 indicated an excess of 2 Da in comparison with that of 6.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze molecular ion differences for compound 7

steps.description: The full MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 7 showed the characteristic peaks of m/z 73, 127, 225, and 255.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze MS/MS spectrum for compound 7 product ions
inputs: MS/MS product ion spectrum of compound 7

steps.description: The configuration of the olefinic bonds was also determined from the biosynthetic pathway, and compound 7 was tentatively assigned as 15hydroxy-12Z-octadecaenoic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: determine olefinic bond configuration for compound 7
